title: Name Files Clearly
summary: Rename files using a clear, consistent format so you can recognize them at a glance.
learnerPromise: You will be able to rename your files using a simple, consistent format that tells you what the file is and when it was created.
whyItMatters: Clear file names save time and reduce frustration. When files are named consistently, you can quickly find what you need without opening random documents to check their contents.
realLifeUses: Rename school assignments so you can tell the subject and due date from the filename
Label photos with the event and date so they are easy to find years later
Name work documents so colleagues and teammates understand what they contain
Organize receipts and invoices with dates and vendor names for easy reference
materialsNeeded: Computer, tablet, or phone with files to rename
result: At least 5 files renamed with a clear, consistent naming format.
youWillLearn: How to choose a naming pattern that includes the topic and date
How to use consistent formatting like hyphens or underscores instead of spaces
How to avoid vague names like 'document1' or 'final_final'
How to apply your naming pattern to both new and existing files
miniChallenge: Choose a simple naming format and rename at least 5 files on your device using it.
steps: Pick a naming pattern you will use, such as Topic-Date-Version or Date-Event-Description.
Find 5 files on your device that have vague or confusing names.
Rename each file following your chosen pattern consistently.
Check that the new names make sense even without opening the files.
Decide to use this pattern for all new files going forward.
completionCriteria: At least 5 files have been renamed using a consistent format
You can tell what each file contains without opening it
You have chosen a pattern to use for future files
commonProblems: Using spaces in filenames which can cause issues on some systems
Writing vague names like 'stuff' or 'thing' that are meaningless later
Adding 'final' to filenames and then creating multiple 'final' versions
tips: Use dates in YYYY-MM-DD format so files sort chronologically
Replace spaces with hyphens or underscores to keep names compatible everywhere
Include a version number like v1 or v2 if you expect multiple drafts
